EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Sandy moved up the Atlantic coast and then turned Northwest and made landfall northeast of MD. Heavy rain and high winds over spread coastal regions and most of Maryland, Northern Virginia, and DC. Heavy rain caused flood and river flooding. As Sandy moved north of the Mid Atlantic, winds switched to southerly and coastal flooding occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Coastal flooding occurred at the Wisconsin Ave gauge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Sandy moved up the Atlantic coast and then turned Northwest and made landfall northeast of MD. Heavy rain and high winds over spread coastal regions and most of Maryland, Northern Virginia, and DC. Heavy rain caused flood and river flooding. As Sandy moved north of the Mid Atlantic, winds switched to southerly and coastal flooding occurred.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Coastal flooding occurred at the Washington Channel gauge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure was located over the central portion of the nation while high pressure remained just off the New England Coast. Surface cold air remained in place during the evening hours of the 22nd into the morning hours of the 23rd. A southerly flow around the low allowed for warm and moist air to overrun the surface cold air, resulting in periods of freezing drizzle. Temperatures rose above freezing later during the morning hours of the 23rd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A light glaze of ice was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Sandy moved up the Atlantic coast and then turned Northwest and made landfall northeast of MD. Heavy rain and high winds over spread coastal regions and most of Maryland, Northern Virginia, and DC. Heavy rain caused flood and river flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rock creek exceeded flood level between 430 pm on the 29th and 1230pm on the 30th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure over the Mid Atlantic led to temperatures to drop below freezing overnight.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Temperatures ranged from 29 to 32 degrees and were reported at a mesonet and Coop station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure across most of the Northeast and Mid Atlantic led to the development of dense fog across the region.  Light winds and saturated low levels aided in fog development.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Viability was reduced to less than a mile at Reagan National Airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure passed through the area during the evening of the 20th into the morning hours of the 21st. There was enough cold air for precipitation to start off as snow, but warmer air eventually wrapped into the system, causing precipitation to change to a wintry mix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Snow and sleet totaled up to one inch near Chevy Chase Village. A glaze of ice accumulation from freezing rain was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure off the coast provided an onshore flow across the area. The onshore flow off the Atlantic Ocean resulted in areas of dense fog.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility was estimated to be around one quarter mile.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper-level disturbance passed through the region in a northwest flow aloft. Extremely hot and humid conditions caused high amounts of instability. The upper-level disturbance triggered a line of thunderstorms that moved through the area. Due to the high instability, thunderstorms caused widespread wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees were down across the District.

The upper-level disturbance triggered a line of thunderstorms that moved through the area. Due to the high instability, thunderstorms caused widespread wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust of 64 mph was measured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Plenty of moisture from the Gulf of Mexico caused high humidity during the 29th. Upper-level high pressure along with sunshine caused extremely hot conditions. The combination of the heat and humidity caused heat indices to be near or above 105 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heat indices were estimated to be around 110 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A northwest flow around Canadian High Pressure caused temperatures to drop below freezing.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Low temperatures were between 31 and 32 degrees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper-level disturbance passed through the region in a northwest flow aloft. Extremely hot and humid conditions caused high amounts of instability. The upper-level disturbance triggered a line of thunderstorms that moved through the area. Due to the high instability, thunderstorms caused widespread wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A wind gust around 51 knots was measured near the U.S. Capitol.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure tracked through the Ohio Valley and into the Great Lakes. The warm front associated with this system passed through our region during the first. Plenty of moisture from the Gulf of Mexico and the Atlantic Ocean combined with strong forcing associated with the warm front, causing thunderstorms to develop. Nearly saturated low levels and training activity supported very heavy rainfall and flash flooding was reported.  The highest rainfall total observed was 2.50 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Blaine Street at 42nd Street was closed and vehicles were trapped.  A nearby rain gauge tallied 2.00 inches.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure tracked through the Ohio Valley and into the Great Lakes. The warm front associated with this system passed through our region during the first. Plenty of moisture from the Gulf of Mexico and the Atlantic Ocean combined with strong forcing associated with the warm front, causing thunderstorms to develop. Moderate instability along with strong forcing caused some thunderstorms to become severe.||Wind speed and direction changed rapidly with height near the warm front. This caused rotating thunderstorms, and some of them were able to produce tornadoes.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple trees were blown down along Kenilworth Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: High pressure was moving off the Mid Atlantic coast overnight. Abundant low level moisture and light winds contributed to fog development across the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility was reported to be around one quarter mile at College Park Airport and Andrews Air Force Base.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the region and showers and severe thunderstorms occurred across the Mid Atlantic. A strong low level jet drove activity through the Interstate 95 corridor and abundant amounts of moisture produced heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down blocking the intersection of 33rd Street and P St NW.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper-level trough and associated cold front moved across Maryland, northern Virginia and eastern West Virginia during the afternoon and evening of the 22nd. High humidity and instability values provided the ingredients for severe storms to develop early in the afternoon and linger into the late evening hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown onto a house.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front move through the Mid-Atlantic during the evening hours. Showers and thunderstorms along the front had very high rainfall rates.  Enough rain fell in the Washington DC to induce an isolated flash flood in the Bloomingdale neighborhood from rainfall amounts of up to 2.36 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding near Rhode Island Avenue and First Street Northwest in Bloomingdale.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front passed through the area on the 8th. High heat and humidity ahead of the boundary caused plenty of instability. The high instability combined with forcing from the front, causing an isolated thunderstorm to produce damaging winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was down due to a thunderstorm wind gust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A nearly stationary low pressure trough induced a slow-moving thunderstorm over Washington DC during the afternoon. Very heavy rain associated with it caused flash flooding in the Bloomingdale neighborhood.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rhode Island Avenue was closed due to flash flooding.  Several vehicles stalled in high water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Hurricane Sandy moved up the Atlantic coast and then turned Northwest and made landfall northeast of MD. Heavy rain and high winds over spread coastal regions and most of Maryland, eastern panhandle of West Virginia and Northern Virginia. Heavy rain caused flooding and river flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts were estimated at Reagan Airport.  There were scattered trees down and power outages.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure drifted eastward across the Mid-Atlantic along a stalled front. Afternoon showers and thunderstorms erupted in a highly moist and unstable atmosphere. Heavy rain associated with some of the activity caused flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple blocks of Rhode Island Avenue reporting flash flooding.  Also, flash flooding forced closures on West Virginia Avenue, Olive Avenue, Eastern Avenue, and Minnesota Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moved through the region and showers and severe thunderstorms occurred across the Mid Atlantic. A strong low level jet drove activity through the Interstate 95 corridor and abundant amounts of moisture produced heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: There was a tree down on 400 block of Olglethorpe St NE.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Low pressure tracked through the Ohio Valley and into the Great Lakes. The warm front associated with this system passed through our region during the first. Plenty of moisture from the Gulf of Mexico and the Atlantic Ocean combined with strong forcing associated with the warm front, causing thunderstorms to develop. Nearly saturated low levels and training activity supported very heavy rainfall and flash flooding was reported.  The highest rainfall total observed was 2.50 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The New York Avenue exit ramp onto South Dakota Avenue was closed due to flash flooding.  A nearby spotter recorded 1.65 inches of rain.
